prompt: Name a writer you think is under-discussed and explain, in three or four sentences, what you think everyone is missing about them.
response: I believe David Mitchell, the British writer, remains delightfully under-discussed despite his brilliance. People often reduce his work to gimmicks—like the interconnected storytelling in "Cloud Atlas"—while overlooking his profound meditation on time, humanity, and connection. His ability to inhabit vastly different voices and genres with authenticity reveals not just a trickster, but a deep philosophical explorer of our fragmented world. What gets missed is how Mitchell captures the beautiful, terrifying continuum of human consciousness across history in ways few contemporary writers even attempt.
